A.title: Requirements (Overleaf, GitHub, REPRO, EDA, Page Length, Legibility, References)

problems.yes: Overleaf and LaTeX: Both a read-only link to your Overleaf is provided and your report is written in LaTeX.
problems.no: One or both requirements are missed.

problems.yes: GitHub Hash: Your final commit hash is provided and your work is accessible.
problems.no: Either you provided no final commit hash or it is inaccessible.

problems.yes: Run Instructions: Appropriate run instructions are provided in your REPRO document.
problems.no: Run instructions are not provided in your REPRO document.

problems.yes: EDA: You provide appropriate EDA in your REPRO document.
problems.no: You don't provide suitable EDA in your REPRO document.

problems.yes: Page Length: You adhered to the stated 8-page length max rule.
problems.no: You neglected to abide by the stated rule that your paper be 8-page max length.

problems.yes: Graph/Text Legibility: Your visualizations and writing adhere to IEEE standard.
problems.no: Visualizations/writing do not comply with IEEE standard and legibility requirements.

problems.yes: Proper References: You provide references outside course materials, appropriately cited.
problems.no: No external references or they are inappropriately cited.

problems.yes: Hypothesis Declaration: At least one context relevant hypothesis is provided.
problems.no: At least one context relevant hypothesis must be provided.

problems.yes: Citing Evidence: Each hypothesis draws support from theory (lecture, paper, proof).
problems.no: Each hypothesis must draw support from theory.

problems.yes: Follow-Through: You provide follow-up to reason whether each hypothesis is proven or disproven.
problems.no: Follow-up must be provided to reason whether each hypothesis is proven or disproven.

problems.yes: Conclusions Defended: You defend observed evidence by citing evidence with interpretation.
problems.no: Must defend observed evidence by citing evidence with interpretation.

problems.yes: EM Hyperparameter Selection: You provide rationale for selection of cluster count.
problems.no: Must provide rationale for selection of cluster count.

problems.yes: EM Results Visualization: You provide visualization to justify written claims.
problems.no: Must provide visualization to justify written claims.

problems.yes: EM Results Interpretation: You provide meaningful interpretation of cluster shape/distribution.
problems.no: Must provide meaningful interpretation of cluster shape/distribution.

problems.yes: KMC Hyperparameter Selection: You provide rationale for selection of cluster count.
problems.no: Must provide rationale for selection of cluster count.

problems.yes: KMC Results Visualization: You provide visualization to justify written claims.
problems.no: Must provide visualization to justify written claims.

problems.yes: KMC Results Interpretation: You provide meaningful interpretation of cluster shape/distribution.
problems.no: Must provide meaningful interpretation of cluster shape/distribution.

problems.yes: RP Component Selection: You justify number of components citing a suitable metric.
problems.no: Must justify number of components citing a suitable metric.

problems.yes: RP Results Visualization: You provide appropriate visualization with citation.
problems.no: Must provide appropriate visualization with citation.

problems.yes: RP Results Interpretation: You provide meaningful analysis (loading scores, feature space, comparison).
problems.no: Must provide meaningful analysis (loading scores, feature space, comparison).

problems.yes: PCA Component Selection: You justify number of components citing a suitable metric.
problems.no: Must justify number of components citing a suitable metric.

problems.yes: PCA Results Visualization: You provide appropriate visualization with citation.
problems.no: Must provide appropriate visualization with citation.

problems.yes: PCA Results Interpretation: You provide meaningful analysis (loading scores, feature space, comparison).
problems.no: Must provide meaningful analysis (loading scores, feature space, comparison).

problems.yes: ICA Component Selection: You justify number of components citing a suitable metric.
problems.no: Must justify number of components citing a suitable metric.

problems.yes: ICA Results Visualization: You provide appropriate visualization with citation.
problems.no: Must provide appropriate visualization with citation.

problems.yes: ICA Results Interpretation: You provide meaningful analysis (loading scores, feature space, comparison).
problems.no: Must provide meaningful analysis (loading scores, feature space, comparison).

E.title: Step 3: Dimensionality Reduction then Clustering (both datasets) (14 points)

problems.yes: Wine Performance Visualization: Appropriate visualization for 6 pairwise combinations.
problems.no: Must provide appropriate visualization for 6 pairwise combinations.

problems.yes: Wine Pairwise Comparisons: Meaningful interpretation of combined effects for Wine.
problems.no: Must provide meaningful interpretation of combined effects for Wine.

problems.yes: Adult Performance Visualization: Appropriate visualization for 6 pairwise combinations.
problems.no: Must provide appropriate visualization for 6 pairwise combinations.

problems.yes: Adult Pairwise Comparisons: Meaningful interpretation of combined effects for Adult.
problems.no: Must provide meaningful interpretation of combined effects for Adult.

F.title: Step 4: Dimensionality Reduction on NN (one dataset) (12 points)

problems.yes: RP Performance Commentary: Evidence of performance improvements for RP.
problems.no: Must provide evidence of performance improvements for RP.

problems.yes: RP Wall Clock Difference: Address speed up in runtime for RP.
problems.no: Must address speed up in runtime for RP.

problems.yes: PCA Performance Commentary: Evidence of performance improvements for PCA.
problems.no: Must provide evidence of performance improvements for PCA.

problems.yes: PCA Wall Clock Difference: Address speed up in runtime for PCA.
problems.no: Must address speed up in runtime for PCA.

problems.yes: ICA Performance Commentary: Evidence of performance improvements for ICA.
problems.no: Must provide evidence of performance improvements for ICA.

problems.yes: ICA Wall Clock Difference: Address speed up in runtime for ICA.
problems.no: Must address speed up in runtime for ICA.

G.title: Step 5: Clustering on NN (one dataset) (12 points)

problems.yes: EM Performance Commentary: Evidence of improvements using EM clusters as features.
problems.no: Must provide evidence of improvements using EM clusters as features.

problems.yes: EM Wall Clock Difference: Relate speed up to filtering effect of EM.
problems.no: Must relate speed up to filtering effect of EM.

problems.yes: KMC Performance Commentary: Evidence of improvements using KM clusters as features.
problems.no: Must provide evidence of improvements using KM clusters as features.

problems.yes: KMC Wall Clock Difference: Relate speed up to filtering effect of KM.
problems.no: Must relate speed up to filtering effect of KM.

EC.title: Extra Credit: Nonlinear Manifold Learning (5 points)

problems.yes: Full implementation with explanation, visuals, and comparison to Steps 1 or 3.
problems.no: Not applicable

problems.yes: Partial implementation with surface-level interpretation but suitable visuals.
problems.no: Not applicable

problems.yes: No demonstrable implementation or only passing remarks.
problems.no: Not applicable
